Wayne, PA · Philadelphia Main Line

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you want to know about Forward Family Medicine and Direct Primary Care — answered plainly. If you don't see your question here, reach out directly →

  • About Forward Family Medicine
  • About Direct Primary Care
  • Membership & Pricing
  • Insurance

  • Getting Started

About Forward Family Medicine

Forward Family Medicine is a Direct Primary Care (DPC) practice in Wayne, PA. We provide comprehensive primary care on a membership model: you pay a flat monthly fee, and in return you receive unlimited primary care visits, direct access to your physician, longer appointments, and wholesale lab pricing — with no copays or insurance billing for covered services. Learn more about our practice →

We are located in Wayne, Pennsylvania, on the Philadelphia Main Line at 295 Old Eagle School Road, Wayne, PA 19087. See our location and contact info →

Dr. David Bigley is the founder of Forward Family Medicine and a board-certified family physician who built this practice to deliver medicine the way it was always meant to be practiced — with time, relationship, and genuine focus on prevention. Dr. Philip Lieberman is dual board-certified in Family Medicine and Lifestyle Medicine, with a culinary arts background that makes nutrition advice actually practical. Meet the team →

Yes — we are actively enrolling new members. Our patient panel is intentionally small, so we encourage you to schedule a Meet & Greet → before we reach capacity.

About Direct Primary Care

Direct Primary Care is a membership-based model of primary care in which patients pay a flat monthly fee directly to their physician — bypassing insurance for primary care services. In exchange, members receive unlimited visits, direct physician access, and longer appointments. At Forward Family Medicine, our panel is capped at 250 patients per physician — versus 2,000+ in traditional practices. Full DPC explainer →

Both models offer smaller panels, direct access, and longer visits. The key difference: traditional concierge medicine typically charges a higher annual retainer and may still bill insurance. DPC does not bill insurance for primary care and charges a lower flat monthly fee — making it more accessible. See the full comparison →

Yes. DPC is legal in all 50 states. Many states have passed legislation explicitly affirming that DPC membership agreements are not insurance products and do not require an insurance license to operate.

We schedule based on need, not volume. On a typical day, Dr. Bigley and Dr. Lieberman may see 6–12 patients — compared to 20–30 in a traditional practice. This is what enables longer appointments, same-day access, and real follow-through on care.

Membership & Pricing

We offer individual, family, and senior pricing with discounts for additional family members. See full membership details and pricing →

Your membership covers unlimited primary care visits (in-person, video, and phone), direct physician access, preventive care, chronic disease management, lifestyle medicine counseling, same-day/next-day scheduling, and minor in-office procedures. Labs and medications are available at wholesale/near-cost pricing as separate add-ons. See full service list →

No. Once you are a member, there are no additional fees for covered visits. The only charges beyond your membership are for labs, medications, and immunizations — all priced at cost.

We offer both month-to-month and annual membership options. Month-to-month plans require 30 days' written notice to cancel. Annual plans include a modest discount.

At Forward Family Medicine, there is no enrollment fee.

HSA/FSA eligibility for DPC membership fees depends on your specific plan and applicable tax rules. Many health-related expenses at our practice (labs, medications, procedures) are eligible for HSA/FSA reimbursement. Please consult your benefits administrator or tax advisor.

Yes. We work with small and mid-size employers to offer DPC memberships as a health benefit. Contact us to discuss employer options →

Insurance

Yes — we recommend it. Your DPC membership provides comprehensive primary care but does not cover hospital stays, emergency care, specialist visits, surgery, or imaging. We strongly recommend maintaining a health insurance plan — ideally a high-deductible health plan (HDHP) paired with an HSA — for these scenarios.

No. We do not bill insurance for primary care services. Your membership fee is the only charge for covered visits. Some members choose to submit their own claims for out-of-network reimbursement — check with your insurer.

DPC membership fees are not reimbursed by Medicare or Medicaid. Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries are welcome to join as self-pay members. Specialist and hospital services would still be billed through Medicare or Medicaid as appropriate.

If you have insurance, you can choose to use it for labs and imaging. However, our wholesale lab pricing is often lower than your insurance-adjusted rate — we'll help you compare and choose.

Appointments & Access

Members typically get same-day or next-day appointments for acute concerns. Routine visits can be scheduled within 24–48 hours. Many concerns are resolved by phone or message without any in-person visit.

Yes. Members have direct access to Dr. Bigley and Dr. Lieberman via phone, text, and secure messaging. You will not reach an answering service or a nurse line.

Monday–Friday, 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM. For urgent matters, members have after-hours contact access for true clinical urgency — not prescription refills or routine requests.

Always call 911 or go to the nearest emergency room for life-threatening emergencies. Your DPC membership is for primary care — not emergency or hospital care.

Yes. Phone and video visits are included in your membership. Many concerns — prescription refills, minor illness, result review, follow-up — can be handled without coming in.

Services & Scope

Yes. We provide care for patients of all ages, from newborns through older adults. Children can be added to a family membership. See membership options →

Yes. Chronic disease management — diabetes, hypertension, thyroid disease, cholesterol, asthma, and more — is a core part of what we do. The DPC model is particularly well-suited for chronic disease management because it provides the time and continuity these conditions require.

We screen for and treat common mental health conditions including depression and anxiety, and provide medication management. For therapy and psychiatric care, we maintain strong referral relationships and help coordinate access.

Absolutely. We work alongside your specialists and serve as the coordinating physician for your overall care — reviewing specialist notes, reconciling medications, and keeping your care integrated.

Yes. Annual physicals, well-woman exams, and preventive health screenings are core services included in your membership.

We perform a range of minor procedures: EKGs, ear lavage, wound care, joint injections, skin tag removal, suturing, spirometry, and more. See full procedures list →

Lifestyle Medicine

Lifestyle medicine uses evidence-based behavioral interventions — nutrition, physical activity, sleep, stress management — to prevent and treat chronic disease. It is integrated into every membership at no additional cost. Learn more →

We don't prescribe one-size-fits-all diets. We use evidence-based guidance — drawing on Mediterranean, DASH, whole-food plant-based, and other well-studied approaches — tailored to your health goals and preferences.

Getting Started

The first step is a free, no-obligation Meet & Greet with Dr. Bigley and Dr. Lieberman. Schedule yours here →

It's a 20-minute conversation — in person or by video — where you can ask questions, learn about the practice, and decide whether Forward Family Medicine is right for you. No pressure, no commitment.

Online enrollment takes about 10 minutes. Your first comprehensive visit is typically scheduled within the first week or two of enrollment.

A list of your current medications, any recent lab results or medical records, and any questions or concerns you'd like to address. We'll have reviewed your submitted health history before you arrive.

Still Have Questions?

We're happy to help. Reach out directly or schedule a Meet & Greet and ask in person — that's what it's for.

Contact Us →

Ready to Take the Next Step?

Start with a free, no-obligation meet and greet. No pressure. No commitment.

Forward Family Medicine is a Direct Primary Care practice. Membership is not insurance. We recommend pairing DPC with a high-deductible or health-sharing plan.
